title: Public Health Notices – Enteric Illness Outbreak Investigations - Canada.ca
content:
## Public Health Notices – Enteric Illness Outbreak Investigations
The Public Health Agency of Canada (PHAC) is responsible for responding to outbreaks of enteric illness (such as Salmonella or E. coli ) transmitted through food, water, contact with animals or other routes. When an outbreak spans more than one province/territory or involves Canada and another country, PHAC works with provincial and territorial health authorities, the Canadian Food Inspection Agency and Health Canada to investigate the source.
Public Health Notices are issued to inform Canadians about outbreak investigations and offer advice on what people can do to protect their health.
To receive email notifications about new outbreak investigations or updates to existing outbreak investigations, please fill out the fields below.
Required fields are marked with an asterisk ( * ).

## Change your email notifications or email address
If you would like to subscribe under a different email, please first unsubscribe under your previous email and re-subscribe under your new email.

## Cancel your subscription
If you are already a subscriber and wish to unsubscribe, type in your e-mail address in the box below and select the  " Unsubscribe " button.
